description: Performs random forest classification on data and class files (cross-validation or test-train prediction), outputting a prediction results file.
author: Omar Halawa;GenePattern Team @ Mesirov Lab - UCSD
plan:
## RandomForest GenePattern Module — Implementation Plan

### 1. Overview
RandomForest is a GenePattern module that Performs random forest classification on data and class files (cross-validation or test-train prediction), outputting a prediction results file.

### 2. Parameters
The module exposes 25 parameters:
- Required: none
- Optional: train.data.file, train.class.file, model.output, model.output.filename, test.data.file, test.class.file, prediction.results.filename, feature.importance.filename, bootstrap, ccp_alpha, class_weight, criterion, max_depth, max_features, max_leaf_nodes, max_samples, min_impurity_decrease, min_samples_leaf, min_samples_split, min_weight_fraction_leaf, n_estimators, random_state, debug, verbose, model.input.file

### 3. Command Line
```
python /RandomForest/rnd_forest.py <train.data.file> <train.class.file> <test.data.file> <test.class.file> <model.output> <model.output.filename> <model.input.file>  <prediction.results.filename>  <feature.importance.filename> --n_jobs <job.cpuCount> <bootstrap> <ccp_alpha> <class_weight> <criterion> <max_depth> <max_features> <max_leaf_nodes> <max_samples> <min_impurity_decrease> <min_samples_leaf> <min_samples_split> <min_weight_fraction_leaf> <n_estimators>  <random_state> <verbose> <debug>
```

### 4. Docker Image
Docker image: genepattern/randomforest\:v0.5

### 5. Validation Strategy
- Validate all required parameters are provided
- Validate file formats match expected extensions
- Check parameter value ranges where applicable

### 6. Implementation Roadmap
Phase 1: Wrapper script implementation
Phase 2: Docker container setup
Phase 3: Parameter validation and error handling
Phase 4: Testing and documentation

parameters.name: train.data.file
parameters.description: Training data (feature) file. Takes .gct file (follows GenePattern GCT format).

parameters.name: train.class.file
parameters.description: Training class (target) file. Takes .cls file (follows GenePattern CLS format).

parameters.name: model.output
parameters.description: Optional parameter to export model trained on the dataset input in "Training Data" as a compressed pickle file (.pkl). Note\: This model will always be fitted using all samples of train.data.file regardless of if LOOCV is carried out for prediction. Only works if training dataset (not model input file) is provided. Default value is False.

parameters.name: model.output.filename
parameters.description: Optional parameter to name the model output file if model.output is True (and training dataset is provided). Default uses training data basename.

parameters.name: test.data.file
parameters.description: Testing data (feature) file. Takes .gct file (follows GenePattern GCT format).

parameters.name: test.class.file
parameters.description: Testing class (target) file. Takes .cls file (follows GenePattern CLS format).

parameters.name: prediction.results.filename
parameters.description: Classifier prediction results filename (.pred.odf, follows GenePattern ODF format). Default is results.pred.odf.

parameters.name: feature.importance.filename
parameters.description: Classifier feature importance filename; only outputted for test-train prediction (.feat.odf, follows GenePattern ODF format). Default is model.feat.odf. See documentation for more details.

parameters.name: bootstrap
parameters.description: Optional boolean to turn on classifier bootstrapping.\n\nDefault value is True.

parameters.name: ccp_alpha
parameters.description:
Optional float for complexity parameter of minimum cost-complexity pruning.

Possible input range is any float greater than or equal to 0.0.
Default value is 0.0.

parameters.name: class_weight
parameters.description: Optional string for class weight specification.\n\nDefault value is None.

parameters.name: criterion
parameters.description: Optional string for node-splitting criterion.\n\nDefault value is "gini."

parameters.name: max_depth
parameters.description: Optional int for maximum tree depth.\n\nPossible input range is ints greater than or equal to 1. Default value is None.

parameters.name: max_features
parameters.description:
Optional string for number of features to consider when looking for best split. "None" will use all features.

Default value is "sqrt."
Note\: "auto" is removed in new version of Scikit (v1.3 and later).

parameters.name: max_leaf_nodes
parameters.description: Optional int for maximum leaf nodes per tree.\n\nPossible input range is ints greater than or equal to 2. Default value is None.

parameters.name: max_samples
parameters.description:
Optional float for ratio of datasets to use per tree.

Possible input range is floats between 0.0 and 1.0 (inclusive of both).
Default value is None.
Note\: If bootstrap is False, can only be None

parameters.name: min_impurity_decrease
parameters.description:
Optional float for minimum impurity decrease needed per node split.

Possible input range is floats greater than or equal to 0.0.
Default value is 0.0.

parameters.name: min_samples_leaf
parameters.description:
Optional int for minimum number of samples required at leaf node.

Possible input range is ints greater than or equal to 1.
Default value is 1.

parameters.name: min_samples_split
parameters.description:
Optional int for minimum sample number to split node.

Possible input range is ints greater than or equal to 2.
Default value is 2.

parameters.name: min_weight_fraction_leaf
parameters.description:
Optional float for min weighted fraction of weight sum total to be leaf.

Possible input range is floats between 0.0 and 0.5 (inclusive of both).
Default value is 0.0.

parameters.name: n_estimators
parameters.description:
Optional int for number of trees in forest.

Possible input range is ints greater than or equal to 1.
Default value is 100.

parameters.name: random_state
parameters.description:
Optional int for seed of random number generator.

Possible input range is non-negative ints (caps at 4294967295, 2^32 - 1). 
Default value is None.
Note\: Setting this to a specific integer for a specific dataset will always yield the same prediction results file; see random_state in documentation for more details.

parameters.name: debug
parameters.description: Optional boolean for program debugging.\n\nDefault value is False.

parameters.name: verbose
parameters.description:
Optional int (0 \= no verbose, 1 \= base verbosity) to increase classifier verbosity. For other values, see Scikit documentation linked above.

Possible input range is non-negative ints.
Default value is 0.

parameters.name: model.input.file
parameters.description: Optional model file input (.pkl, similar to model.output file). This can serve as a substitute for "Training Data," and if both are provided, the model input file is used.
